data.narrative:
Intramotev is a Series A drone and autonomous systems company founded by Tim Luchini, Alex Peiffer, and Corey Vasel, having raised $14.4M to date. The Drones & Autonomous sector comprises 8 identifiable private peers including Zipline, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air, with no public comparables available for direct benchmarking.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable valuation method given data constraints. Using total capital raised ($14.4M) as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $29M. This method rests on observable step-up patterns between funding rounds but does not incorporate company-specific operating metrics or market position relative to peers.

Peer-median methodology is not applicable: while five same-sector companies exist, the narrow filters of stage-matching, adjacent-stage proximity, and comparable valuation tier yielded insufficient datapoints (requiring ≥3). Broadening to all-sector medians would introduce unacceptable noise given heterogeneous applications and business models across the drone ecosystem.

The triangulated valuation range is $24M (low) to $33M (high), with a median of $29M. This range reflects stage-progression mechanics only and carries material uncertainty around capital efficiency, unit economics, and competitive positioning relative to the peer set—factors not captured by the available data. The sparse comparable universe limits confidence in any point estimate.
